evals.prompt: Install GitHub CLI on this Windows machine, get GitHub authentication working, and make sure future GitHub publishing from the terminal is set up correctly.
evals.expected_output: Inspect the machine state, install gh if needed, choose a reliable authentication path, and summarize the final setup state plus any required user-auth checkpoint.

evals.prompt: This folder I want to publish is nested inside another unrelated Git repository. Create a clean publish repo from just this subfolder, push it to GitHub, and avoid touching the parent repo.
evals.expected_output: Detect that the current folder should not reuse the parent repository, create a clean sibling publish repo, and push only the intended content.

evals.prompt: I already have packaged .skill files and a local repo, but git push and gh release commands are unreliable. Publish the repository anyway and create a release with both assets.
evals.expected_output: Use the most reliable available path, including GitHub API fallback if needed, and return the repo URL plus the release URL.
